claim: Ollama structured outputs using Pydantic (Python) or Zod (JavaScript) enforces output schema; this is superior to JSON mode which often returns invalid JSON or unstructured text
actionable: Use structured outputs with schema definitions instead of JSON mode; this guarantees valid structured data and enables downstream processing without validation overhead
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs

claim: Named entity recognition results are inconsistent across models and runs; Llama 3.2 vision model misses entities like GPT-4 names in same text depending on prompt
actionable: Test NER models thoroughly on your domain data; don't trust initial results. Add system prompts mentioning target entity types to improve extraction reliability
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs

claim: Structured output field descriptions in Pydantic improve model extraction accuracy; detailed descriptions of what to extract help models focus on relevant information
actionable: Add descriptive text to schema fields; the more explicit the field purpose, the better the model understands what to extract from images/text
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs

claim: Ollama can respond as OpenAI endpoint (not just native SDK), enabling drop-in replacement for models currently using OpenAI API without code changes
actionable: Configure Ollama as OpenAI-compatible endpoint to migrate existing OpenAI-based code to local models; reduces dependency on closed-source APIs
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs

claim: Vision model performance varies significantly by model and image quality; smaller quantized models (Llama 3.2 11B) work locally but may miss details on blurry/complex documents
actionable: Test local vision models (11B, 13B) on representative images from your domain; performance degrades on low-quality documents, requiring larger models or preprocessing
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs

claim: Fine-tuning vision models on domain-specific tasks (e.g., coin counting, license plate reading) significantly improves accuracy without requiring larger base models
actionable: For specialized vision tasks, fine-tune smaller models on your domain data rather than relying on larger generic models; this improves accuracy and reduces inference costs
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs

claim: Running Ollama locally provides privacy for sensitive document processing; images never leave the machine, enabling HIPAA/compliant document processing without external APIs
actionable: Deploy Ollama locally for processing sensitive documents (medical, financial, legal); this maintains data privacy and enables compliance without external service dependencies
source_title: Building a Vision App with Ollama Structured Outputs
